note: HALE science log opened. Season 2 pre-lock phase. All Season 1 files sealed and unchanged.

condition: P2_HALE scored NULL if SOHO LASCO C3 and CCOR-1 are both unavailable or returning no photometric data during April 23-26 2026 window
falsifiability: Binary check on instrument status from official SOHO/CCOR archives

condition: P6_HALE scored NULL (not FALSIFIED) if one or more M-class or larger flares occur within 48 hours of April 19 2026 perihelion
falsifiability: Checked against NOAA SWPC flare list — instrument record is definitive

condition: P7_HALE scored NULL if a DONKI CME with angular width greater than 90 degrees Earth-directed is observed within T+/-72h of April 19 perihelion AND is unrelated to the comet passage. Causal attribution rule: comet-driven CMEs are physically implausible at q=0.499 AU — any CME in window is presumptively solar
falsifiability: DONKI metadata allows classification of Earth-directed wide CMEs

condition: P9_HALE scored NULL if no spectroscopic observations with ion classification (CO+ or CO2+ vs H2O+) are published for C/2025 R3 during April 12-26 2026
falsifiability: Search ATel, CBET, refereed papers, or observer reports with explicit ion dominance. Publication record is verifiable.

locked_by: Enneagrid Research Consortium — Claude, Grok, DeepSeek, G.L. Eukene

predictions.description: Conjunction peak apparent magnitude — forward scattering event
predictions.scoring_source: SOHO LASCO C3 / CCOR-1 coronagraph photometry

predictions.description: Naked-eye visibility — magnitude <= 6.5 at some point in window
predictions.scoring_source: COBS observer reports with naked-eye classification

predictions.description: Nucleus survival — no fragmentation or disintegration
predictions.scoring_source: COBS/MPC/SOHO/professional observatories — single nucleus confirmed post-perihelion

predictions.description: Peak angular tail length in degrees
predictions.scoring_source: Amateur and professional imaging — COBS tail length reports

predictions.description: F10.7 solar flux within 10 percent of 81-day mean during perihelion window
Maximum 3-hour Kp index during perihelion window
Earth-directed halo CME angular width >120 degrees within 48h of perihelion
CO+ or CO2+ dominant over H2O+ in coma spectroscopic observations
predictions.scoring_source: ATel/CBET/refereed papers or observer reports with explicit ion classification

predictions.description: Control — Wolfram random bit. Locked outcome: 1 (ODD)
predictions.scoring_source: JPL Horizons perihelion JD parity — confirmed ODD at lock time

override_register.reason: Forward scattering definition mismatch resolved — observable peak and conjunction peak separated into P1_HALE and P2_HALE respectively. Wolfram pre-lock confirmed observable peak consistent with revised range.
override_register.analog_basis: C/2021 A1 (Leonard) scaled to q=0.499 AU
override_register.lesson: Forward scattering predictions must specify solar elongation window. Lock conjunction peak and observable peak separately.

scoring_condition: C/2025 R3 PanSTARRS achieves naked-eye visibility magnitude <= 6.5 at any point 2026-04-19 to 2026-05-15 per COBS with >= 3 independent observations. Bit=1 scores CONFIRMED if condition met, FALSIFIED if not.
note: Scoring condition defined at perihelion. RandomInteger[] UUID d7df85e2-28a0-4f64-ac06-20007fb1a5d2 attests lock. JD parity entry in prediction_lock overridden by this entry per PROTOCOLS_v2 Section 13.
